page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The multi-core stress-ng CPU test scored 311843.21, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 21353.49 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 483.46 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also demonstrate top-tier throughput, with Redis handling 359.39 million operations per second. However, the instance exhibits weaker relative performance in low-parameter large language model prompt processing.

Given its massive physical core count and high memory bandwidth, the m8gb.48xlarge is highly resource-dense, offering qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the 200 Gbps network interface.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64-based AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It delivers 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it provides a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database and Redis operations. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM prompt processing fall into average and lower tiers. This resource-dense configuration is highly suited for parallelized workloads, including large-scale database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-volume static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-V2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is an ARM64 server with 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 platform delivering 192 dedicated physical cores and exceptional memory bandwidth for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory and a 200 Gbps network interface
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
